steps.title: Translate the yearly change
steps.content: Each year, the account has $1.3\%$ more than it had the previous year. That means the old balance is multiplied by $1+0.013$, so the yearly multiplier is $$1+0.013=1.013$$

steps.title: Classify the pattern
steps.content: Repeated multiplication by the same factor gives an exponential model, not a linear model. A linear model would add or subtract the same dollar amount each year, but a percent increase adds an amount based on the current balance.

steps.title: Check the direction
steps.content: The multiplier $1.013$ is greater than $1$, so the balance grows as time increases. The model is increasing, and the repeated multiplication makes it exponential. The answer is C.
